218.reason: [Same issue as above: after detecting a single '|', the lexer records an error but does not return early or skip the invalid character, leading to potential infinite loops or misalignment in parsing. Also, curPos is not advanced past the erroneous '|', so the same character will be reprocessed indefinitely.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: after detecting a single '|', the lexer records an error but does not return early or skip the invalid character, leading to potential infinite loops or misalignment in parsing. Also, curPos is not advanced past the erroneous '|', so the same character will be reprocessed indefinitely.]

304.reason: [Loop condition should be curPos < source.length(), not source.length() - 1, because when curPos == source.length() - 1, the last character is still unread. This causes the final character (e.g., a single '&' or '|' at EOF) to be skipped, leading to missed error detection or incomplete tokenization.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop condition should be curPos < source.length(), not source.length() - 1, because when curPos == source.length() - 1, the last character is still unread. This causes the final character (e.g., a single '&' or '|' at EOF) to be skipped, leading to missed error detection or incomplete tokenization.]
